HRPG.SpeedExceeded: The bug exceeded its available Speed this turn.
HRPG.StaminaExceeded: The bug spent more Stamina than it had available.
HRPG.ShortDescriptionHint: Appearance, personality, and role in a few sentences
HRPG.SkillListHint: A Skill contains four skill names. Matching names across different Skills add their ranks together, capped at 3 dice from ranks.
HRPG.ChooseBugTemplateHint: Choose a bug type and apply its template first. Attributes, traits, and equipment will then become available.
HRPG.Name: Bug Name
HRPG.SizeApplyFailed: Could not apply the size template. Details were written to the console.
HRPG.AbsorptionHint: Absorption roll: Shell; successes are usually 5–6.
HRPG.DefenseActionHint: Defense is taken in response to an attack, costs 1 Stamina, and is resolved as either Dodge or Parry. Stamina recovers at the start of the bug's turn.
HRPG.DefenseActionDescription:
A general reminder for reacting to an incoming attack. In most cases, choose Dodge or Parry next, because those buttons make the actual defensive roll.
The first 1 Stamina pays for the reaction and is not invested into the roll. Extra Stamina can add dice according to the chosen defense.
HRPG.DodgeHint: A Grace check to avoid an attack. Invested Stamina adds dice based on the attacker's weapon weight; jumping bugs may use Speed.
HRPG.DodgeDescription:
Dodge can be used against normal attacks, spells, and area attacks if the bug is not immobilized.
If the dodge removes all attack successes, the attack misses. If it has at least 1 success but does not fully avoid the attack, it is a partial dodge.
When dodging, the bug can move 1 cell without provoking an attack, but gains 1 Imbalance. Against area attacks, this movement is usually required.
HRPG.ParryHint: A Power roll with a shield or weapon. Add shield/weapon quality; ranged attacks usually apply -1 die.
HRPG.ParryDescription:
Parry usually answers a melee attack with a weapon or shield. Spells and area attacks usually cannot be parried unless a rule says otherwise.
Add the Quality of the weapon or shield being used. Parrying a ranged attack takes a -1 die penalty.
When parrying with a weapon, each extra invested Stamina usually gives 2 dice instead of 1, but the maximum investment is limited by the weapon's Quality.
HRPG.DamageAbsorptionHint: After expected damage is calculated, the defender rolls Shell; each success reduces damage by 1.
HRPG.DamageAbsorptionDescription:
Absorption is rolled after the attack hits and expected damage is known.
Each Shell success reduces damage by 1. Some damage types or flask effects can bypass Absorption; in that case, follow the item, trait, or technique text.
HRPG.TechniqueEffectHint: What the technique does, what it improves, and what conditions it requires
HRPG.TechniqueWrongMartialPath: You picked an art that does not match the character's martial Paths.
HRPG.TechniqueWrongMysticPath: You picked a mystery that does not match the character's mystic Paths or rank.
HRPG.NoTraits: This character has no traits yet.
HRPG.WeaponQualityHint: Natural weapons have base Quality 1 and light weight. Mark a lost weapon as Quality -1; after a full-Satiety rest it regenerates to 0, and damaged weapons restore 1 Quality after each rest.
HRPG.NoDice: At least one die is required.
HRPG.AttackTaxHint: Current Stamina tax for this attack: {tax}. Tax is spent, but does not add dice unless an ability says otherwise.
HRPG.AttackStaminaSpent: Stamina: base cost {base}, invested {invested}, tax {tax}, total spent {total}.
HRPG.DodgeMoveImbalance: Moving after a dodge does not provoke attacks, but gives 1 Imbalance.
HRPG.OpportunityAttackHint: An attack against a provoking bug costs 1 Stamina; that point is not invested into the roll. A bug can make this attack against each other bug once per round.
HRPG.OpportunityAttackDescription:
Use this when an enemy provokes an attack: for example, leaving a dangerous position or taking an action the GM rules as exposing them.
This button records the reaction cost. Make the actual attack with the weapon or natural attack button in the Skills page.
HRPG.RetreatHint: Spend 1 Stamina and up to 2 Speed to move without provoking attacks. After retreating, the bug gains 1 Imbalance.
HRPG.RetreatDescription:
Retreat is used to safely leave danger without provoking an attack.
Default cost: 1 Stamina and 2 Speed. The system adds Imbalance afterward because the bug withdraws from an unstable position.
HRPG.DashJumpHint: Spend 1 Stamina to move 2 cells in any direction. A jump can be extended with Speed according to movement rules.
HRPG.DashJumpDescription:
Use this for a burst of movement, a jump over an obstacle, or reaching a better cell.
The base dash/jump costs 1 Stamina. If the bug needs to go farther, enter the extra Speed spent in the action field.
HRPG.GrappleHint: A grapple is a Power attack. Add unarmed or suitable weapon Quality and up to 2 bonus dice for additional appendages.
HRPG.GrappleDescription:
Grapple is an attacking action used to grab a target and restrict its movement.
This button rolls a Power action and spends Stamina. If traits, weapons, or circumstances help, add their bonus dice manually.
HRPG.EscapeGrappleHint: A grappled bug spends 1 Stamina and tries to escape with Power or Grace; extra Stamina can be invested.
HRPG.EscapeGrappleDescription:
Use this when the bug is already grappled and wants to break free.
Choose Power for a forceful escape or Grace for a nimble escape. Extra Stamina can be entered as bonus dice.
HRPG.SkillActionHint: A combat skill action usually costs 1 Stamina: hide, search, intimidate, predict, climb, and so on.
HRPG.SkillActionDescription:
This is a generic button for unusual combat actions that are not attacks: hide, scan the battlefield, intimidate, hold on, help an ally, or perform a trick.
Choose the fitting attribute and add bonus dice if a skill, trait, item, or situation helps.
HRPG.MinorActionHint: A minor action costs no Stamina, but spends Speed equal to twice the item's or obstacle's Weight, minimum 1.
HRPG.MinorActionDescription:
A minor action covers brief interactions: draw an item, open a door, pick something up, switch gear, or move a light obstacle.
It usually costs no Stamina. If it involves an item or obstacle, enter the Speed spent.
HRPG.ReadyActionHint: Spend 1 Stamina and name a trigger. When it happens, the bug interrupts and performs the readied action, paying its normal cost.
HRPG.ReadyActionDescription:
Ready an action when the bug waits for a specific event: 'if the enemy approaches', 'if the door opens', or 'if my ally attacks'.
This spends 1 Stamina for readying. When the trigger happens, pay the normal cost of the readied action separately and write the trigger in the note.
HRPG.DelayTurnHint: A bug can delay its turn. When the new turn arrives, Stamina spent between delay and the new turn is not recovered.
HRPG.DelayTurnDescription:
Delay moves the bug's turn to a later point in initiative.
Use it to wait for an ally, an opening, or an enemy action. Remember: Stamina spent between delaying and the new turn is not recovered a second time.
HRPG.FocusSoulHint: A Focus action ends the turn; its effect resolves at the start of the next turn unless the bug spends Stamina or Soul before then.
HRPG.FocusSoulDescription:
Soul Focus is a risky way to restore Hearts. The bug invests Soul, ends its turn, and waits until the start of its next turn.
If the bug spends Stamina or Soul before Focus resolves, Focus is broken. If it is not broken, roll the invested Soul: each success restores 1 Heart.
Insight grants Focus rerolls, which the system shows in the roll card.
HRPG.FocusActionHint: Focus resolves at the start of the next turn and is broken if Stamina or Soul is spent before then.
HRPG.FocusSoulSpent: Soul invested: {cost}. Each success restores 1 Heart when Focus resolves.
HRPG.DamageCalculatorHint: Calculates expected damage: base damage, extra successes, DR, Absorption roll, and Absorption pool.
HRPG.DamageCalculatorDescription:
This is not a character action; it is a helper for manual attack resolution.
Enter attack successes, base damage, damage reduction, Absorption successes, and available Absorption pool. The system reports the expected final damage.
HRPG.DamageCalcMiss: The attack missed: no successful dice.
HRPG.NoPathAbilities: No matching path abilities for this attack.
HRPG.ChoosePathFirst: Choose a path from the list first.
HRPG.PathAlreadyAdded: This path is already on the character.
HRPG.PathNotFound: The path was not found in the system catalog.
HRPG.PathDescriptionHint: Describe the Path's philosophy and style
HRPG.TraitAlreadyAdded: This trait is already on the character.
HRPG.TraitNotFound: The trait was not found in the system catalog.
HRPG.TraitRequiresSmallSize: This trait is available only to Small bugs.
HRPG.TraitCatalogFailed: Could not open the trait catalog. Details were written to the console.
HRPG.ItemCatalogFailed: Could not open the item catalog. Details were written to the console.
HRPG.ItemCatalogNotFound: The item was not found in the system catalog.
HRPG.ItemDescriptionHint: Item description, rules, notes, or flavor text
HRPG.FluidVialEffectHint: For the Fluids subtrait, choose a common vial effect or an uncommon effect for +2 Hunger.
HRPG.NeedlePathWeaponGrace: Path of the Needle: this weapon uses Grace for the attack.
HRPG.HookPathWeaponGrace: Path of the Hook: this weapon uses Grace for the attack.
HRPG.MagicFocusInsightAttack: Magic focus: this weapon uses Insight for the attack.
